notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
support model counts one beneficiary and active-ability heals/shields only (passive heals, CC, and damage-reduction utility are not scored)
the sim opens on Stonewall, which the field rarely builds (2.4% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Adele (it never builds Dawnstar, Xenia) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for physical survival: first spike lands around minute 14 (Stonewall), third item by minute 28. Take Lotus: +16.6% on your damage rotation at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: Physical-Def Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 5,690 effective HP vs magical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 2% of our best core on magical survivability. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.title: Physical-Def Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 8,089 effective HP vs physical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +51% more physical survivability: try Stonewall over Dynamo (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Physical Bruiser (Physical-Def)
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 25 — about 78 auto-attack DPS in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Zinx's main threat is Ricochet; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Phase's main threat is Energy Lance; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Dekker deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Riktor ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.
They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Mourn's main threat is Abduct; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Narbash deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

laneSteer.provenance: “Court of Roses” ⇒ sustain; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field support 51.2% over 30,531 games, magnitude not simulated.

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 24 — about 459 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Steel deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 22% of its bite; you give up 28% of your damage.
